text: Eesayuu nìn ká yii Isaaki mēē ki bīiǹ Jaakɔb boon ki bí yii ú nīn cá Padan-Aram kí cáá unimpu niin, ní ki tīī-u mara yii ú taa cáá unimpu Kanaan yab sapɔmbi kansikin.
translated: Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Padan-Aram to get a wife there, and had commanded him not to take a wife from among the Canaanite women.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: U kpēēyee, lɔkɔ ubɔ dɔ̄ akpaanni tipicaŋkpun tita dɔ̄ ki kɔ́ĺ. Niin ní bipikpaaliibi nìn lúù nnyim ki nyùntī ipii. Ditaŋkpakpaandi diba nìn pīī lɔkɔee nyɔkɔpu.
translated: Long ago, there was a river where many hippos lived. It was there that the children used to go to fetch water. A certain crocodile used to live at the mouth of the river.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «Tiŋman, a baasii sá m-niliu ní kiŋkiŋ.» Jaakɔb nìn kāl̀ Laaban cee ki sāā uŋmal ubɔ. Jaakɔb cáá Leeya nì Raseel
translated: And Laban said to Jacob: «Truly, you have been a great help to me.» Jacob then stayed with Laban to work for another year. Jacob had Leah and Rachel.

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Dalbee ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «A nín sá m-niliuee puee, á nīn tūǹ ditundi míǹ faalaa ki tìì-maaa? An puee tùkù-mi kun a là ḿ dāá pà-see.»
translated: Then Laban said to Jacob: "Since you are my relative, should you work for me for nothing? Therefore, tell me what you want me to pay you.

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Ní Jaakɔb nì Raseel mun kpáfì. Jaakɔb nìn néeń Raseel ki jítiǹ Leeya, ní ki nìn gíti kāl̀ abin aluli ki túń ditundi ki tīī Laaban. Jaakɔb biyaamu bɔti
translated: And Jacob and Rachel met. Jacob loved Rachel and hated Leah, and he again stayed seven years and worked for Laban. Jacob's many children.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Leeya nìn dūu cáá dipool ki màl̄ kibininjabiki, ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Rubeen. Di sá yii un nìn lī yii: «Ti-Dindaan ká m-falaau, kɔŋkɔnnee m-cal làá nīn kíǹ ki néeń-mi.»
translated: Leah conceived and gave birth to her firstborn son, and she named him Reuben. She said, 'The Lord has seen my affliction; now my husband will love me.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Kujoou Jaakɔb nyánní kusaau ki kùnnée ní Leeya túŋkì-u nsanni ní ki bí-u yii: «Man cee ní a làá dòon̄ díǹ. Kí ŋmā kí ká-si á dòon̄ m-ceeyee, m-jipɔɔn mandragɔɔr biliŋ ní m dūu pàn̄ m-yɔnti.» Ní Jaakɔb lafun dōoǹ u-cee kunyeeu gbanti.
translated: And Jacob went out to the field to meet her, and Leah met him and said to him: «You will come in to me tonight. For I have hired you with my son

text: Di sá yii kí wàatī ḿ bàńee, tin a nìn cááyee kaa nìn náaǹ báà fiii. Ama kí yòon̄ ŋwiin ŋun m-taal tāā a-dumpuee, a-wanti wíiǹ tikpil ní ti-Dindaan bīiǹ a-boon. Too, kɔŋkɔnnee ŋyunti ŋulɔŋu ní man làá gíti dūú tɔ̄ ditundi kí ká tin làá ŋmàntī ḿ kpèè m-dicaŋkuee?»
translated: If you waited for me to be ready, what you wanted did not happen at all. But for the day that I have been waiting for you, your patience has been great, and our

ref: Jacob cut branches from some poplar trees and from some almond and evergreen trees. He peeled off part of the bark and made the branches look spotted and speckled.
text: Ní Jaakɔb bátì isufi in bi yíì peepiliiyee nì amandi nì plataanee pambiyaamu nsɔmu, ki cùkū cùkū-mu ki māntìi tú ki dāa kátì taapu, ní ki pɔ́kɔtì ajan ŋin u cùkūee, ní ndɔsɔmuee kíǹ ki cáá mpim mpim.
translated: And Jacob took the stone that they called a pillow, and gold and silver, all his possessions, and he poured oil on it repeatedly, and made it stand up as a pillar, and he

ref: When the stronger sheep were mating near the drinking place, Jacob made sure that the spotted branches were there.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun ní tiwaŋkuti tin yáàyee yaa làá jɔ̄m̀ tɔbee, ní Jaakɔb ń yòon̄ní ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee kí ŋá tiwan tin poon tiwaŋkuti nyùee kí túĺ ti-nimbiin, ní tì jɔ́ḿ tɔb ndɔsɔmu mun u cùkūee cee.
translated: The day the young ones were about to fight each other, Jacob prepared the food he had cooked to give to the young ones to eat, and they all ate the food he had cooked.

ref: Once, when the flocks were mating, I dreamed that all the rams were either spotted or speckled.

translated: The time when they were doing good things, I saw among them that others were doing bad things secretly and with envy and with hatred.

ref: So he took some of his relatives along and chased after Jacob for seven days, before catching up with him in the hill country of Gilead.
text: Niinee ní Laaban yōoǹ u-nabiyaamu ní bi nín jì Jaakɔb boon. Bi cōom̀ iwiin iluli ní ki tin nín cúú-u Galaadi jool pu.
translated: Then Laban pursued his relatives and caught up with Jacob's people. They camped for seven days and then reached him on Mount Gilead.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Ama un cee ní a yaa ká a-waaŋee, bí kpɔ̀-u. Ti-nabiyaamu kɔkɔ di bí nee. Dāan̄ m-cee kí kpèè a yaa ká a-wanti tiba á yòon̄.» Jaakɔb kaa nìn nyí yii Raseel di yùn̄ awaa.
translated: But the one with whom your stolen gods are found, let him die. All our possessions are yours. Come and search, if anything of yours is found, take it." Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.

ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: Tiba di yaa cúú a-waŋkuluuku kubɔee, maa cāabìń-si diwaŋkpil, see ḿ nyàab̀ kí téeǹ kun an cúúyee yil. A-waŋkuti tin bi yù ŋwiimpuee nì tin bi yù kunyeeuee, a bàlifī-mi ní ḿ téeǹ-si.
translated: When the chief dies, the elders gather to choose a new chief, and I will help them to choose the one who will be chief. The elders who are wise and those who are strong, I will help them to choose.

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: M pátì kí fōō tiŋan tin a ŋá-mi nee nì tibulcin tin a dàkā man a-tɔntɔnliu nee, kun puee ŋkpandɔ baba ní m nìn ŋúb́ ki dūtì Jɔɔdan buŋŋu nee. Ama díǹ pu nee, m yākatì m-nib nì m-waŋkuti akiti ali ní nee.
translated: I have finished receiving the lands that you gave me and the instructions that you showed me for my service, because for forty years I led them across the Jordan River. But now, I have

text: Ní Jaakɔb bí-u yii: «M gbāam̀-si, tùkù-mi a-yindi.» Ní u bí-u yii: «Ba pu ní a là kí béé m-yindi?» Niin niin ní u bīiǹ Jaakɔb boon.
translated: Then Jacob said to him: "I beg you, tell me your name." Then he said to him: "Why do you want to know my name?" Nevertheless, he blessed Jacob.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ní u kpáafì un līīyee yii: «M-maan Eesayuu yaa tú-si nsanni ní ki yaa bālifì-si yii: <A-kpiliu sá ŋma ní a cá la? Ŋma di yì tiwaŋkuti tin līī a-nyɔkɔpu nee?>,
translated: And he asked the woman, saying: "My son Isaiah will ask them and will answer them, saying: 'Who is your king and where are you going? Who will remove the thorns that are in your feet?

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.

translated: And Jacob went on his way. He looked, and God's angels met him.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Ní u kpáafì un līīyee yii: «M-maan Eesayuu yaa tú-si nsanni ní ki yaa bālifì-si yii: <A-kpiliu sá ŋma ní a cá la? Ŋma di yì tiwaŋkuti tin līī a-nyɔkɔpu nee?>,
translated: And he asked the disciples, saying: "My master Isaiah will send them and will ask them, saying: 'Who is your king and where are you going? Who will remove the thorns that are in your eyes?

ref: The man said, “From now on, your name will no longer be Jacob. You will be called Israel, because you have wrestled with God and with men, and you have won.”
text: Ní unaaciinee bālifì-u yii: «Bi yíì-si yii ba ní?» Ní u kíí-u yii: «Bi yíì-mi yii Jaakɔb ní.»
translated: And the man asked him saying: «What is your name?» And he answered him saying: «My name is Jacob.»

ref: As Jacob was on his way back home, some of God's angels came and met him.
text: Kutaa wúntì an dáá bɔ̀ǹee ní Laaban yíkì ki kpàl̄ u-bisalib nì u-naantiibi ki kāaǹ-bi, ki mēē ki bīiǹ bi-pu ní ki fātìi nín kùǹ u-dumpu. Jaakɔb bɔ́ntì kí tūŋkì Eesayuu
translated: And in the morning Laban rose and kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them, and bid them farewell and then returned to his home. Jacob sent to Esau.

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ní Jaakɔb mun nín nìkì u-saŋŋu. U cée ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi cílìń u-pu nsanni.
translated: And Jacob went on his way. He saw that God's angels met him.

ref: Afterwards, Jacob went back and spent the rest of the night alone. A man came and fought with Jacob until just before daybreak.
text: U cáá u-yab kɔkɔ ki dūtì mbun ki kpáaǹ tin kɔkɔ u cááyee.
translated: He took all his possessions, went to the market, and sold everything he owned.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Ní unaaciinee bí-u yii: «Baa làá ti yíiń-si Jaakɔb, bi làá nīn kíǹ ki yíì-si yii Isirayeel ní. Di sá yii si nì Unimbɔti mɔ̀n̄ ní si nì binib mun mɔ̀n̄, ní a pɔ́ɔkiǹ bi-kɔkɔ.»
translated: And the man said to him: "No longer will they call you Jacob, but they will now call you Israel. Because you have struggled with God and with men, and you have overcome them all.

ref: “But, brother, I already have plenty,” Esau replied. “Keep them for yourself.”
text: Ní Eesayuu kíí-u yii: «M-nabiki, m cáá tigaajati tikpil, dū a-wanti kí kpāafì.»
translated: And Esau said to him: «My brother, I have many possessions; take your things and go.»

ref: Jacob's other sons came and took everything they wanted. All this was done because of the horrible thing that had happened to their sister.

translated: And Jacob sent to the land to bury his dead and to mourn them there, because they were afraid of their enemies.

ref: They took sheep, goats, donkeys, and everything else that was in the town or the countryside.
text: Bi nìn cúú bi-piii nì bi-ŋoobui nì bi-naai nì bi-mammu nì tin kɔkɔ nìn pée bí kitiŋ poon nì tisatinee.
translated: They had their wives, their sons, their daughters, their livestock, and all things that were in their land and their possessions.

ref: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “Look what you've done! Now I'm in real trouble with the Canaanites and Perizzites who live around here. There aren't many of us, and if they attack, they'll kill everyone in my household.”
text: Ní Jaakɔb bí Simeeyɔn nì Leefii yii: «Ni kɔ́ǹ-mi tibɔtin! Ni náań kudɔ̀ŋu ki bíl̀ man nì kitiŋ nee nib kansikin ní nee, an dàkà yii Kanaan yab nì Peeriisi yab. Man nib kaa náaǹ. Bíǹ ŋūǹ kí kpāfì kí dāá jáǹ-mi ní kí kúntí m-dicaŋku kɔkɔ.»
translated: Then Jacob said to Simeon and Levi: "You have troubled me, making me odious among the inhabitants of this land, that is, the Canaanites and the Perizzites. My

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Eesayuu cáá Kanaan tiŋkin ní binimpoobi. Ada un nìn sá Eelɔn, ueetija bisalee nì Ooliibama un nìn sá Ana bisal nì Siibeeyɔn ueefija naantiiuee.
translated: Esau had the land of Canaan and his descendants. These were Elon, his wife, and Oholibamah, who was Anah's daughter, and Zibeon, his wife.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Míǹ pu ní Eesayuu, un bi gíti yii Eedoomee, cūtìi kóòń Seeyiir jool pu. Eesayuu naantiibi
translated: And Esau, who is Edom, settled on Mount Seir. Esau's descendants.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Diisan jipɔmbi di sá Usi nì Aran.
translated: They built houses for Usi and Aran.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Ní uninjee bí-u yii: «Bi yíkì doo, di sá yaadalee m nìn gbìl̄ bi bíi lì yii: <Tí yīkìmaan doo kí nīn cá Dootan.>» Ní Jooseefi nín jì bi-boon Dootan ní ki tin ká-bi niin.
translated: And the young man told him: "They have left, and I heard them saying: 'Let's go to Dothan.'" So Joseph went after them to Dothan and found them there.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Boonee ní u gíti dámintì ní ki tí dūu máafì u-mambi yii: «M tí dámintì kudamintiiu kubɔ. M dámintì yii ŋwìiń nì uŋmal nì iŋmalbijaa saalaa nì ubɔ dòoń ki gbàantí m-nimbiin.»
translated: Boonee then swore again and took a knife in his hand, saying: «I will swear an oath. I swear that the sun, the moon, all the stars, and God will come to bear witness to my words.»

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ní u-baa bí-u yii: «Á cù kí kpèè yii a-mambi pɔ̄ɔ̄ɔɔ, bi-waŋkuti mun bí mana, kí gítiń kí tùkù-mi.» Eebrɔn tantakalin ní bi nìn bí ní Jooseefi baa tú-u ní u yíkìi búntì Siikeem tiŋkin u-mambi cee.
translated: And his father said to him: "Go and see if your brothers are well, if the flocks are well, and come back and tell me." They were in the valley of Hebron when Joseph's father sent him, and he went to Shechem to look for his brothers.

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Bi nín ká-u u páaǹní ndan kaa láá bāań-bee, ní bi sòon̄ ki bɔ́ntì yii bí kpɔ̀-u.
translated: You saw him suffering there, and you didn't help him, then you went and killed him.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.

text: Ní Juuda bí Oonan yii: «Fōō a-maan nimpuu ukpiipu kii mara nín pée sá puee, kí màal̀ kí téeǹ u-yil u-dicaŋku ń taa kúntí.»

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: U-cal Pootifaar dāa bāańee ní u gíiǹ ki téeǹ míǹ mbaantiim yii: «Ueebruja uyumbu un a cáań-tee dɔ́miń kí dòon̄ nì mi.
translated: Potiphar's wife saw him and said to him these words: "This Hebrew man whom you brought to us came to insult me and lie with me.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Ní ujanjakpilee lēē Jooseefi ú nīn bí bi-cee ki tútúǹ-bi atuun. Bi nìn yúntì sarka poon ŋwaam. Jooseefi lēē bisarkadambi bili damintiiti taapu
translated: And the captain of the guard had Joseph, his slave, doing work for him. They had put them in prison. Joseph was with two important prisoners.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Ama m gbāam̀-si, bi yaa fātìi fōō-si ní a-pɔbil sɔ̄ŋkèe, a mun ń téetí kí ŋá-mi tiniti kí sòoǹ m-bɔti kí tùkù Faaroo kí nyāntí-mi sarka nee poon.

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: Man nì u nìn dámintì kunyabaantiiu poon ní báà ŋma nì u-damintiiku nì ku-taapu.
translated: I and he were friends in the village until death, with his friends and their families.

ref: The king sent for Joseph, who was quickly brought out of jail. He shaved, changed his clothes, and went to the king.
text: Niinee ní Faaroo tú bi yíiń Jooseefi. Ní bi nyántì-u kusarkadiin malaa, ní u kōol̀ ki kpántì u-wampeeŋkaati ní ki nín cá ubɔti nimbiin.
translated: Then Pharaoh sent for Joseph. And they quickly brought him out of the dungeon, and he shaved and changed his clothes, and then went before the king.

ref: I saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river, and they began feeding on the grass.
text: M yúee ní ki ká inasal iluli, i bí màlmal ki ŋān̄, ki nyánní dimɔɔlin ki dɔ́miń ki yú digbándi ki ŋmóò timool.
translated: I went and found a big tree, it was very green, and it had many fruits, and I picked them and ate them and felt good.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Ní Unimbɔti yíiń kugbɔŋuee yii kutagbɔŋu. Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwinliliiti dal dee.

text: Ní kunyeeu mɔ́ ní kutaa dūu wúntì, ŋwintataati dal dee.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

translated: And God created a person and made him his own; he created him to be God's own. He created a man and a woman.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.

translated: God finished his work, and on the seventh day, he rested and blessed all the works he had done.

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Kutagbɔŋu nì kitiŋ náantam nín kíĺ puee dee. Eedeen kpaabun bɔti Ŋyunti ŋun ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti nyántì kitiŋ nì kutagbɔŋuee,

text: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti síiǹ unil mara nee yii: «A ŋūǹ kí ŋmɔ́ isufi in yú bukpaanee kɔkɔ biliŋ,
translated: And our Lord God called someone and said: "You can count all the stars in the sky.

text: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti bí ukoobu yii: «An nín ŋá pu ní a ŋá mimmee, m tɔ́-si mbusu tiwaŋkuti nì ipeel kɔkɔ kansikin. A làá nīn kíǹ ki fù a-bindi pu ní. A-jinti di làá nīn kíǹ ki sá ŋkpɔŋkpɔm kí tin sāā a-kúm dal.
translated: And the Lord God said to the serpent: "Because you have done this, I curse you more than all livestock and all wild animals. You will continue to crawl on your belly. And

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Ní u bí unimpu yii: «A nín ŋá miŋyee, m làá gbéeǹ-si timatíǹdi gìńdi. Nì falaa ní a làá nīn màal̀ a-biyaamu. A-cal neeliŋu làá nīn pée cáá-si, ama úǹ di làá nīn cáá sii pu mpɔn.»
translated: And he said to the woman: «Because you have done this, I will greatly increase your pains. And in pain you will give birth to your children. Your desire will be for your husband, but he will rule over you.»

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.

translated: And a ninja killed the snake for Eve, so that he would not be a murderer, and also so that he would not be a murderer of all people.

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: U ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki bí yii: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, unil kpántì kii ti-kansikin ubɔ dee, ki kíǹ ki nyí tiŋan nì tikpitil. Tí taa yàntī ú ŋmɔ́ dimaŋfal subil kí nīn bí jaanjaan kaa kúù.»
translated: He then said to them: «Kɔŋkɔnnee, a person who is stronger than us, and he knows the land and the forest. We must not let him gain great power and then be free and not die.»

translated: And Abram said to him: «I swear by my hand and pray to our Lord, the Almighty God who created heaven and earth.

translated: And the Lord said to him: "I am the Lord who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to possess it.

translated: And he pitched his tent and went south, and Abram moved his camp.

text: An pu ní bi yíì lɔkɔee yii: «Beer Lakayi Rooyi», taapu di sá yii <Un bí dimaŋfalin ki wāĺ-mee lɔkɔu.> Lɔkɔ gbanti dɔ̄ Kadeesi nì Beereedi timmu kansikin.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Ní Abraam bí Unimbɔti yii: «Too, á pɔ̄ Isimaayeel mmaŋfafɔkɔn!»

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «M gbìl̄, ama an kaa sá Isimaayeel bɔti ní m bíi sòoǹ, an sá a-nimpuu Saara di làá màal̀-si kibininjabiki. Á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Isaaki. Úǹ nì u-naantiibi bin làá páanní boonee di làá fōō dipoondi din m pōoǹ dí nīn bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.
translated: And God told him: "I have heard, but it is not about Ishmael that I am speaking; it is that your wife Sarah will bear you a son. You shall name him Isaac. He and his descendants who will come after him will receive the covenant that I have made and will continue to exist forever.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: A nín mēē-mi Isimaayeel pu miŋyee, m gbìl̄. M làá bìin̄ u-boon kí yàntī ú màal̀ ki bùtī tikpil tikpil. U làá dāá màal̀ bibɔtiibi saalaa nì bili, ní u-maaliŋu ń dāá nīn sá ditimbuŋkpaandi.
translated: You have heard me concerning Ishmael. I have listened. I will bless him so that he may multiply and become very great. He will beget twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation.

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Unimbɔti sòon̄ nì Abraam ki dóòyee, ní ki yíkì u-cee ki nín jɔ́ḿ.
translated: When God had finished speaking with Abraham, he ascended from him.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: Ŋwimbaanti gbanti ní u gíí ki kpáaǹ bin kɔkɔ pée sá bininjab ki bí u-cindinee, iyumbu nín màl̄ biŋyee nì bin u dáal̀ binicambi ceeyee.
translated: The great chief went and gathered all the men to be his subjects, the women who bore children, and those he had raised as young men.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: U dāa yáatì ki kpēē nyɔkɔpuee, bininjab bita di yú kaa dátiǹ-uee. U ká-bee ní ki yíkì u-tantiu nyɔkɔpu ki sáǹ ki túŋkì-bi. U bàn̄ bi-ceeyee, ní ki gbāaǹ ki jáam̀-bi.
translated: He went out to meet the people, three men who had come to him. He saw them and went back to his household to prepare to serve them. He came to where they were, and knelt down

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Abraam tùkū u-nimpuu mimmee, ní ki sáǹ ki cūtìi kɔ́ u-nacaŋkpuŋkun, ki cúú dinajapɔmbil din bí màlmalee ki tīī u-tɔntɔnliu ní úǹ kūū-dì ki ŋɔ́fì malaa malaa.
translated: Abraham called all his men, and he sent his servants, and they took fat young goats and gave them to his servant, and he killed them and roasted them thoroughly.

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Abraam nì u-nimpuu nìn púĺ tikpil, u-nimpuu nìn jítì mmaal pu.
translated: Abraham and his wife were very old; his wife had stopped bearing children.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Ní Abraam sútì ki pìl̄ ti-Dindaan ní ki bí-u yii: «Mimmee, a làá kúntí binikpitib kí kpáaǹ deedeedambaaa?
translated: Then Abraham approached and said to the Lord, 'Will you indeed sweep away the righteous with the wicked?

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Ti-Dindaan sòon̄ nì Abraam míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki búntì, ní Abraam mun nín fàtī u-dumpu.
translated: The Lord spoke with Abraham, and He remembered what He had done, and Abraham also remembered his servant.

ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Niinee ní ti-Dindaan yāntì farwata nyánní yilpu ki fōō ŋŋmi ki sìtī kii utaayee Soodoom nì Goomoora timmu pu.
translated: And then our Lord caused a whirlwind to come out of the sky, and it took fire and thus destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: U nìn nāaǹ ntimu gbanti nì ditantakal din poon mu bée kɔkɔ, ki kūū ki céetì mu-nib nì isufi nì timool kɔkɔ.
translated: He was doing these things with great effort, as you all know, and he completed them and presented them to your people with joy and complete peace.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Kutaa wúntèe ní ukpil tùkū u-naal yii: «M fu dōoǹ ti-baa cee kunyeeu. Tí gíti nyùntī-u ndaam kunyeeu nee si mun ń cùú dòon̄ u-cee, tí ŋmā kí tīī ti-baa inibool.»
translated: The old man then told his son: "Go and bring our father's hoe. Then go and bring him the hoe again, and if he goes to bring it, then we will give our father a gift.

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Ní ki gíti bí yii: «Ba di yāntì ní a ŋá míǹ?»
translated: And he asked them, saying: «What have they done and what are you doing?»

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Niinee ní Abimeeleeki cúú inaa nì ipii nì iŋoobu nì iyumbu bininjab nì binimpoobi ki tīī-u, ní ki gíiǹ u-nimpuu Saara mun ki tīī-u.
translated: Then Abimelech took sheep and oxen and male servants and female servants and gave them to him, and also returned his wife Sarah to him.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Nì min kɔkɔ Abraam nìn púĺ tikpilee, Saara cáá dipool ki màl̄-u kibininjabiki ŋyunti ŋun Unimbɔti nìn pée yóoǹ Abraamee deedee.
translated: When Abraham was very old, Sarah conceived and bore him a son at the exact time God had promised Abraham.

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: Ní ki sútì ki cūtìi kāl̀ ŋkpaanceeti, kun puee u lī u-neen yii: «Maa là kí ká kibiki nee nín làá kpú puee.» An puee u kāń ndan ní ki sūǹ ki yīkintí.
translated: So he went out and went to the peanut field, and then he said to his wife: «I will go to cut this plant that will die.» And then he took a hoe and went to dig

text: U nìn kóò kupɔɔu kun nìn bí Paaran tiŋkinee ní u-na dāa cáá unimpu Eejipiti ki tīī-u. Abraam nì Abimeeleeki pūtì ki pōoǹ tɔb
translated: He dwelt in a place which was in the land of Paran, and his wife took a woman from Egypt and gave her to him. Abraham and Abimelech made a covenant with each other.

ref: And so, Abraham promised he would.

translated: Then Abraham took sheep and cattle and gave them to Abimelech, and they made a covenant with each other.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Ní Abraam cóoǹ busubu bun bi yíì yii tamariisee Beerseeba ní ki jáam̀ ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti un pée bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.
translated: And Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and called upon the Lord God, the Everlasting One.

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Ní u-baa kíí-u yii: «M-biki, Unimbɔti u-ba di làá tin tīī-ti dipibil din ti làá dūú ŋá saraawɔkaayee.» Ní bi nín nìkì bi-saŋŋu.
translated: And his father said to him: "My child, God will give us a child whom we will make a sacrifice." And they returned to their home.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Bi bàn̄ laakin Unimbɔti dàkā-bi bí cùee, ní Abraam máń niin nsaraafan, ki kɔ́fì idɔ ki páaǹ ní ki bóob́ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki ki páaǹ idɔee pu.
translated: They went to the place God had shown them, and Abraham built an altar there, and arranged the wood and laid it, then bound his son Isaac and laid him on the wood.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Abraam ŋá míǹ ki dóòyee ní ki tāntì u-ŋal ki yōoǹ kijiki kí bàà làntì u-jipɔɔn.
translated: Abraham said so and swore, and he raised his hand to make an oath that he would bless his offspring.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Kpalaayee ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu téenní yilpu ní ki bí yii: «Abraam! Abraam!» Ní u kíí yii: «M sèé».
translated: The angel of the Lord appeared to him and said: "Abraam! Abraam!" And he answered: "Here I am.

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Ní ti-Dindaan tuuŋŋu tí yíinní Abraam yilpu nliliitiim
translated: And through our Lord, we called Abraham, his son, and his descendants.

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: «Aayee, ukpil! Pīlifiǹ-mi, maa làá nyāfiǹ-si kusaau nì ntaŋkpalɔkɔ ŋun bí ku-poonee. M làá dūú pɔ̄-si ní m-nabiyaamu kɔkɔ nimbiin, á cù kí síb́ a-nimpuu.»
translated: Alas, Chief! Forgive me, I will show you the man and the woman who are inside. I will lead you and all my people to your mother's place, for you to

translated: And then Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave of the field of Machpelah, facing Mamre, which is Hebron, in the land of Canaan.

text: Dalbee, Abraam yíiń u-tɔntɔnliu un yúntì u-cee ki sá ukpil ki kpèé u-wanti kɔkɔee ní ki bí-u yii: «Dū a-ŋal kí tíkiń m-tapandi taapu.

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.

ref: “I thank you, Lord God of my master Abraham! You have led me to his relatives and kept your promise to him.”
text: ní ki bí yii: «M nyɔ́ŋkí ti-Dindaan, m-kpiliu Abraam Nimbɔtiu un ŋáań-u tiŋan múlmul kaa gbèentée! Úǹ di tí kpōotì-mi ki cáań ki kɔ́ǹ m-kpiliu nabiyaamu cindin táb.»
translated: and they said: «My servant Dindaan, my master Abraham's God, who made him prosper greatly and did not abandon him! He will protect me and lead me to my master's relatives' land.»

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: U gíti bí-mi yii: <A yaa cūtì m-dumpu, m-cindi poon ní baa kíí kí tīiń-si usapɔɔnee, a nyáń dipùtil din m yāntì a pūtèe bɔtin.>
translated: He said to me: <If you had followed my advice, I would have been able to help you with the Europeans, and you would have gotten the rifle I gave you for nothing.>

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Ní usapɔɔn niija nì u-na bí-u yii: «Yàntī usapɔɔn ń gíti kàl̄ ti-cee iwiŋkaaŋkaa kii iwiin saalaa pu. Míǹ boonee ní á nín kpáaǹ-u kí búntí.»
translated: And the servant went to his master saying: «Let the servant first count our sheep for three days. After that, you can kill him and skin him.»

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”

translated: And it came to pass that Rebekah's womb struggled within her, and she said: "Two nations are in your womb. The older will serve the younger!

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Ní Isaaki yōoǹ Reebeeka ki cáa kɔ́ǹ u-na Saara tantiun, ní ki dū-u ki kpántì u-nimpuu ní ki néeń-u. An di ŋméeǹ Isaaki u-na kúm.
translated: And Isaac loved Rebekah and brought her into his mother Sarah's tent, and took her to be his wife, and was comforted by her. This happened after Isaac's mother died.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: U nìn màl̄-u Siimran nì Yɔkisan nì Meedan nì Madiyan nì Yiisbaaki nì Suuwa.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: ní ki pú u-nimpoobi bin u nìn gíti cáá boonee biyaamu mun tiwan tiba. Ŋyunti ŋun Abraam bí kaa láá kpíìyee ní u yāntì u-nimpoobee biyaamu yíkì ki búntì ndan, ŋwìiń nyalaŋki pú, kí nīn dátiǹ u-jipɔɔn Isaaki. Abraam kúm
translated: And then he gave his concubines, with whom he had had children, gifts and goods. Before Abraham was about to die, he made his concubines' children move away from there, to the east, to leave his son Isaac. Abraham died.

ref: Isaac was the son of Abraham,
text: Abraam jipɔɔn Isaaki biyaamu mun u màl̄ee bɔti sèé. Abraam màl̄ Isaaki.

translated: And the Philistines swore an oath and confirmed all the wells that his father Abraham's servants had dug during his lifetime there.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Ní u-tɔntɔnliibi gíti gbìn̄ lɔkɔ ubɔ ní bi tí dɔ́miń ki táká-bi, ní Isaaki pú lɔkɔ gbanti mun diyindi yii Siitina, taapu di sá yii <Bi jáń nì mi.>
translated: And his servants went and dug another well, and they quarreled with them, and Isaac named that well Sitnah, because it means 'They opposed me.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Ní Isaaki bí-bi yii: «Naa là m-bɔti ní ki jìn̄-mi ni-tiŋkin, ni gíti nyàab̀ ba m-cee doo?»
translated: And Isaac said to them: "How can I bless you and give you land, when you have already sold what I gave?

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: Ku wúntì kutaafaauee ní bi pūtì ki pōoǹ tɔb. Míǹ boonee ní Isaaki nyántì-bi nsanni, ní bi nín kùǹ nì dipɔɔsɔŋkil.
translated: And when the preparations were ready, they put them together. After that, Isaac gathered them all, and they ate with joy.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: Ní ki dū tijin tin u ŋée nì kpɔnɔb bin u púĺee ki dūu ŋúbiǹ Jaakɔb ú cáá tīī u-baa.
translated: And he took the gifts that he had prepared and the servants that he had sent ahead to meet Jacob, his brother.

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Ní u sútì ki pìl̄ u-baa ní u-baa máfì máfì-u, ní ki bí yii: «Nneen sá Jaakɔb neen, ama ní iŋal sá Eesayuu ŋalii.»
translated: And he came out and met his father, and his father embraced him tightly, and he said: «The voice is Jacob's voice, but the hands are Esau's hands.»

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: U jíń ki dóòyee ní ki bí Jaakɔb yii: «M-jipɔɔn, sútiń kí dāá kpàl̄-mi.»
translated: He woke up and said to Jacob: "My son, get up and bless me.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: <Unimbɔti ń tīī a-kpaatanti nsɔŋfi ŋun nyēeń yilpuee, kí yàntī a-tiŋki ń nīn gàm̀ kí lòoǹ tiŋan, tikpaajin nì ndamam ń gbóotí a-cee.
translated: May God bring shame upon your enemies, those who are against you, and may He cause your land to continue to prosper and bear fruit, and may blessings and good things follow you.

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Ní Eesayuu bí yii: «Bi nín yíì-u yii Jaakɔb <Uŋmatinlee> pu ní u líbitì-mi míǹ mili kɔkɔɔɔ? U nìn fōō m-kpiti, ní kɔŋkɔnnee u tí fōō m-biindi. Mimmee, m-baa, aa kíǹ-mi dibiindi dibaaa?»
translated: And Esau said: "Is he not rightly called Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times! He took my birthright, and now he has taken my blessing. Therefore, my father

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: An puee m-biki, á pīlifì m nín làá sāŋkì-si puee! Yīkìí sāǹ kí nīn cá m-niija Laaban cee, Kaaran tiŋkin.
translated: I will give my daughter, you will choose the one I will give to you! Go and stay with my brother Laban, in Haran.

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Á nīn bí niin kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun a-maan ŋuul làá dāá sɔ̀ŋkèe.
translated: You will be the one to eat for three of those days, your food will be enough for you.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Ní ki bí-u yii: «Yīkì kí nīn cá Padan-Aram, a-na baa Beetuweel cee kí kpèè a-wee Laaban sapɔmbi kansikin ubɔ kí cáá.
translated: And he said to him: "Go to Padan-Aram, and go to Bethuel's house to take one of Laban's daughters for yourself.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Eesayuu nìn ká yii Isaaki mēē ki bīiǹ Jaakɔb boon ki bí yii ú nīn cá Padan-Aram kí cáá unimpu niin, ní ki tīī-u mara yii ú taa cáá unimpu Kanaan yab sapɔmbi kansikin.
translated: Esau saw that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him away saying he should go to Padan-Aram to get a wife there, and had commanded him that he should not take a wife from among the daughters of Canaan.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: U dɔ̄ee ní ki dámintì yii akɔl yú kitiŋ ki tin sāā kutagbɔŋu ní Unimbɔti tuuŋyi tà ki jɔ́ḿ ki kpákatiń.
translated: He saw it and thought that the work of the land that was causing destruction was spoiling and ruining God's work.

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: U kpēēyee, lɔkɔ ubɔ dɔ̄ akpaanni tipicaŋkpun tita dɔ̄ ki kɔ́ĺ. Niin ní bipikpaaliibi nìn lúù nnyim ki nyùntī ipii. Ditaŋkpakpaandi diba nìn pīī lɔkɔee nyɔkɔpu.
translated: Once upon a time, a certain tree had a hole where three small birds lived. There, young birds were born and grew up. A certain snake used to frequent the tree's hole.

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Taa ŋūǹ ki ŋá míǹ, asee ipii kɔkɔ yaa bāań ki kpáfì. Niinee ní tí nín cúú ditaŋkpal kí bíntí ní kí nyùntī-i.»
translated: And they answered him saying: «Don't do that, otherwise all the birds will come and eat it. So we will take a net to cover it and protect it.»

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Bi yú ki sòoǹ míǹ ní Raseel dūu bāań nì u-baa piii, di sá Raseel nìn sá upikpaal ní.
translated: They say that Rachel was born with a lot of hair because Rachel was a hairy person.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «Tiŋman, a baasii sá m-niliu ní kiŋkiŋ.» Jaakɔb nìn kāl̀ Laaban cee ki sāā uŋmal ubɔ. Jaakɔb cáá Leeya nì Raseel
translated: And Laban said to Jacob: «Indeed, you have been a great help to me.» Jacob stayed with Laban to serve him further. Jacob had Leah and Rachel.

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Dalbee ní Laaban bí Jaakɔb yii: «A nín sá m-niliuee puee, á nīn tūǹ ditundi míǹ faalaa ki tìì-maaa? An puee tùkù-mi kun a là ḿ dāá pà-see.»
translated: Then Laban said to Jacob: "If you are my relative, will you then serve me for nothing? Therefore, tell me what your wages should be.

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Ní Laaban kóoǹ niin yab kɔkɔ ki kpáfì ní bi jíń tinimpucan jiŋaaliu.
translated: Laban went among all the people and searched, and they found the household gods.

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Ní Jaakɔb nì Raseel mun kpáfì. Jaakɔb nìn néeń Raseel ki jítiǹ Leeya, ní ki nìn gíti kāl̀ abin aluli ki túń ditundi ki tīī Laaban. Jaakɔb biyaamu bɔti
translated: So Jacob and Rachel came together. Jacob loved Rachel and neglected Leah, and then he stayed again for seven years and worked for Laban. Jacob's many children.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Leeya nìn dūu cáá dipool ki màl̄ kibininjabiki, ní ki pú-kì diyindi yii Rubeen. Di sá yii un nìn lī yii: «Ti-Dindaan ká m-falaau, kɔŋkɔnnee m-cal làá nīn kíǹ ki néeń-mi.»
translated: Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben. For she said, "The Lord has seen my affliction; surely now my husband will love me.

ref: and they had a son.
text: Ní Biila cáá dipool ní ki màl̄ Jaakɔb kibininjabiki.

translated: When he was about to finish the first part of the story, the second part said to him: "Wait, you will tell the third part next.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Eesayuu cáá Kanaan tiŋkin ní binimpoobi. Ada un nìn sá Eelɔn, ueetija bisalee nì Ooliibama un nìn sá Ana bisal nì Siibeeyɔn ueefija naantiiuee.
translated: Esau had children in the land of Canaan. One of them was Adah, his wife, daughter of Elon, and Oholibamah, who was the daughter of Anah, and Zibe

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.
text: Míǹ pu ní Eesayuu, un bi gíti yii Eedoomee, cūtìi kóòń Seeyiir jool pu. Eesayuu naantiibi
translated: So Esau, who was called Edom, settled on Mount Seir. Esau's descendants.

ref: Zibeon's sons were Aiah and Anah—the same Anah who found an oasis in the desert while taking the donkeys of his father out to pasture.
text: Siibeeyɔn jipɔmbi di sá Aya nì Ana. Ana gbanti di sá un dāa nìn ká dinyimbɔbil diba kuteeun ŋyunti ŋun u nìn bíi kpàá u-baa Siibeeyɔn mammuee.
translated: Siibeeyɔn's children are Aya and Ana. Ana, however, is the one who experienced a great misfortune when she was taking care of her father Siibeeyɔn in his old age.

ref: Dishan's sons were Uz and Aran.
text: Diisan jipɔmbi di sá Usi nì Aran.
translated: They built houses for Usi and Aran.

ref: “They're not here anymore,” the man replied. “I overheard them say they were going to Dothan.” Joseph left and found his brothers in Dothan.
text: Ní uninjee bí-u yii: «Bi yíkì doo, di sá yaadalee m nìn gbìl̄ bi bíi lì yii: <Tí yīkìmaan doo kí nīn cá Dootan.>» Ní Jooseefi nín jì bi-boon Dootan ní ki tin ká-bi niin.
translated: And the young man told him: "They have left, and it is true that I heard them saying: 'Let us go to Dothan.'" So Joseph went after them to Dothan and found them there.

ref: His brothers asked, “Do you really think you are going to be king and rule over us?” Now they hated Joseph more than ever because of what he had said about his dream.
text: Niinee ní u-mambi kíí-u yii: «Mimmee a dàkafī yii sii di làá nīn sá ti-bɔtiu deeyaaa, àá sii di làá nīn sá ti-kpiliu ní timbi ń nīn bí sii taapu ní?» U-damintiiku gbanti nì u nín sòon̄ puee yāntì bi-náŋki mɔ̄tìi wíiǹ u-pu.
translated: Then his child asked him, saying: "Father, do you think that you will be able to be our elder forever, or will you be able to be our leader and we will be with you?" The old man was surprised by what he said, and his heart was moved towards him.

ref: Joseph later had another dream, and he told his brothers, “Listen to what else I dreamed. The sun, the moon, and eleven stars bowed down to me.”
text: Boonee ní u gíti dámintì ní ki tí dūu máafì u-mambi yii: «M tí dámintì kudamintiiu kubɔ. M dámintì yii ŋwìiń nì uŋmal nì iŋmalbijaa saalaa nì ubɔ dòoń ki gbàantí m-nimbiin.»
translated: Boonee swore again and went to gather his people, saying: «I have sworn a great oath. I swore that the sun, the moon, and all the stars, and the earth will come to bear witness to my words.»

ref: His father said, “Go and find out how your brothers and the sheep are doing. Then come back and let me know.” So he sent him from Hebron Valley. Joseph was near Shechem
text: Ní u-baa bí-u yii: «Á cù kí kpèè yii a-mambi pɔ̄ɔ̄ɔɔ, bi-waŋkuti mun bí mana, kí gítiń kí tùkù-mi.» Eebrɔn tantakalin ní bi nìn bí ní Jooseefi baa tú-u ní u yíkìi búntì Siikeem tiŋkin u-mambi cee.
translated: And his father said to him: "Go and see if your brothers are well, and if the flocks are well, and return and tell me." They were in the valley of Hebron when Joseph's father sent him, and he went to Shechem to look for his brothers.

ref: But before he got there, they saw him coming and made plans to kill him.
text: Bi nín ká-u u páaǹní ndan kaa láá bāań-bee, ní bi sòon̄ ki bɔ́ntì yii bí kpɔ̀-u.
translated: If you find him busy, and you cannot help him, then you will talk and decide to kill him.

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Ŋyunti ŋun Rubeen dāa cūtì kí nyāntí Jooseefi dilɔkɔgindinee, u bàn̄ee u kpá, ní an wùn̄-u tikpil ní u dáaĺ tiwan tin u pēēyee ki cátì.
translated: When Reuben returned to the pit to look for Joseph, he arrived and found it empty. It pained him greatly, and he tore his clothes and left.

ref: Meanwhile, the Midianites had sold Joseph in Egypt to a man named Potiphar, who was the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Ŋyunti gbantee Madiyan yab bíǹ dūu bàn̄ Eejipiti ní ki mun nìkī Jooseefi ki nyáfiǹ Pootifaar, ujanjakpil un cí Faaroo cindee.
translated: Some time later, the Midianites brought Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, an officer who was the captain of Pharaoh's guard.

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: Ŋyunti gbantee, Juuda yíkì u-nabiyaamu cee ki búntì Adulaam tiŋkin kijapaai kiba cee, bi yíì-u yii Yiira.
translated: After this, Judah went down from his brothers and turned aside to a certain man of Adullam, whose name was Hira.

ref: While there he met the daughter of Shua, a Canaanite man. Judah married her,
text: Niin ní Juuda ká ukanaanja Suuwa bisal ki cáá ní u nì u kpáfì.
translated: Then Judah and his brothers sold Joseph completely, and he was gone.

ref: So Judah told Onan, “It's your duty to marry Tamar and have a child for your brother.”
text: Ní Juuda bí Oonan yii: «Fōō a-maan nimpuu ukpiipu kii mara nín pée sá puee, kí màal̀ kí téeǹ u-yil u-dicaŋku ń taa kúntí.»
translated: And Judah said to Onan: "Take your brother's wife and marry her, and raise up offspring for your brother, so that his name may not die out.

ref: Hirah asked the people of Enaim, “Where is the prostitute who sat along the road outside your town?” “There's never been one here,” they answered.
text: Ní u bālifì binib bin bí niinee yii: «Kinimpulaakpaai kin kàal̀ Eeniyayiim tiŋkin doo, nsan caŋinee bí la?» Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Kinimpulaakpaai kiba pée kpá doo.»
translated: And he asked the people who were there: "The Kinimpulaakpaai in Eeniyayiim village, what is its purpose?" And they answered him: "The Kinimpulaakpaai is just for killing.

ref: “Those are mine!” Judah admitted. “She's a better person than I am, because I broke my promise to let her marry my son Shelah.” After this, Judah never slept with her again.
text: Juuda béè tiwanee ní ki bí yii: «U cáá ibaamɔn ki jítiǹ man. An bàà sá ḿ dū-u kí tīī m-jipɔɔn Seera, ní maa ŋá míǹ». Ní Juuda kaa ti pée gbēē-u.
translated: Judah then told the woman, saying: "He has a child who is growing up. It will be that I will give him to my daughter Seera, and I will do so." And Judah did not listen to her at all.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Ŋyunti ŋun u bíi màal̀ee ní jaasibee kansikin ubɔ nyántìń u-ŋal, ní umatimmaanli dū gulfaman ki kúĺ, ní ki bí yii: «Un bí nee di péé ki nyáń.»
translated: While he was speaking, one of the scribes stretched out his hand, and the teacher took a pen and wrote, and said: "The one who is here will come out.

ref: Potiphar liked Joseph and made him his personal assistant, putting him in charge of his house and all of his property.
text: An puee Jooseefi nìn ká diyimɔɔl u-kpiliu cee u dū-u ki kpántì úǹ u-ba tɔntɔnliu, ní ki dū-u ki ŋá ukpil ú nīn kpèé u-cindi poon tuuŋŋi nì tin kɔkɔ u cááyee.
translated: And Joseph's master saw that he was a great dreamer, and he took him and made him his servant, and he made him the overseer of all the work in his house and everything he owned

ref: Then she said, “That Hebrew slave of yours tried to rape me!
text: U-cal Pootifaar dāa bāańee ní u gíiǹ ki téeǹ míǹ mbaantiim yii: «Ueebruja uyumbu un a cáań-tee dɔ́miń kí dòon̄ nì mi.
translated: Potiphar's wife saw him and said to him these words: "This Hebrew man whom you brought to us to mock me and lie with me.

ref: They spent a long time in prison, and the official in charge of the palace guard, made Joseph their servant.
text: Ní ujanjakpilee lēē Jooseefi ú nīn bí bi-cee ki tútúǹ-bi atuun. Bi nìn yúntì sarka poon ŋwaam. Jooseefi lēē bisarkadambi bili damintiiti taapu
translated: And the captain of the guard had Joseph, who was his servant, doing work for him. They were also imprisoned in the guardhouse. Joseph had two important prisoners with him.

ref: One night each of the two men had a dream, but their dreams had different meanings.
text: Bi bí sarkan niin ní kunyeeu kubɔee, un pūul̀ Eejipiti bɔtiu kpɔnɔbee nì un bàtī ndaam ki tìì-uee liitil dámintì, báà ŋma nì u-damintiiku nì ku-taapu.
translated: They were kings and high priests. He ruled Egypt as a great king and wore a crown made of small diamonds, even with its diamonds and their settings.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Kutaa wúntèe Jooseefi dɔ́miń bi-cee ní ki ká yii an kaa mɔ̄ǹ-bi.
translated: When Joseph saw his brothers, he knew that it was them.

ref: “We each had a dream last night,” they answered, “and there is no one to tell us what they mean.” Joseph replied, “Doesn't God know the meaning of dreams? Now tell me what you dreamed.”
text: Ní bi kíí-u yii: «Ti dámintì ní ubɔ kaa bí kí tùkù-ti taapu.» Ní Jooseefi bí-bi yii: «Unimbɔti di kaa tùkù tidamintiil taapuɔɔɔ? Míǹ puee māafìmaan-mi ni nín dámintì puee.»
translated: And they answered him saying: "Our dream is that no one can interpret it for us." And Joseph said to them: "Does God not interpret dreams? Therefore, tell me your dream.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: An kíǹ iwiin ita Faaroo làá nyāntí-si sarkan kí gíiǹ a-tundi kí ŋúbíǹ-si, ní á tīī-u tinyɔkaal kii a nín nìn pée kpààa tìì-u puee.
translated: It will come to pass that Pharaoh will oppress them severely, to make them work and to afflict them, and you will give him tribute just as you used to give it to him before.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Ama m gbāam̀-si, bi yaa fātìi fōō-si ní a-pɔbil sɔ̄ŋkèe, a mun ń téetí kí ŋá-mi tiniti kí sòoǹ m-bɔti kí tùkù Faaroo kí nyāntí-mi sarka nee poon.
translated: But I beg you, if I find favor in your sight, you will remember to show me kindness, and speak well of me, and tell Pharaoh, and get me out of this prison.

ref: Three days later, while the king was celebrating his birthday with a dinner for his officials, he sent for his personal servant and the chief cook.
text: Iwiin ita dalee, an dūu ŋmāntiǹ Faaroo maaliwiiŋŋu jiŋaaliu, ní u yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi kɔkɔ jiŋaal. U yāntì bi nyántì bin bàtī ndaam ki tìì-uee kpiliu nì bin pūul̀-u kpɔnɔbee kpiliu ki cáań u-tɔntɔnliibi kɔkɔ nimbiin.
translated: On the third day, he brought Pharaoh's chief baker to trial, and he brought all his servants to trial. He made them bring the chief cupbearer and the chief baker before all his servants.

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: U yú mimmee ní ki ká inasal iluli, i bí màlmal ki ŋān̄, ki nyánní dimɔɔlee poon ki yú digbándi ki ŋmóò timool.
translated: He saw a woman who was cooking inasal outside, she was very beautiful, wearing a skirt, and she had a big bottom, shaking her waist.

ref: Later, seven other heads of grain appeared, but they were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: U kpēēyee ní ki ká ndifiŋkpiimu mululi mun ugbuŋkoon fáá mu fáaḿee nyáń ki kútì idifin in béeyee pu.
translated: He looked and saw a very large forest, with all its trees and plants, which then ended at the river there.

ref: When you were angry with me and your chief cook, you threw us both in jail in the house of the captain of the guard.
text: Di sá dalbee, a nìn fōō timbi a-tɔntɔnliibi pu diŋuul ki cáá man nì bin pūul̀-si kpɔnɔbee kpiliu ki tɔ́ sarkan ujanjakpil un kpèé a-sarkadambee dumpu.
translated: A long time ago, they used to take great care of their servants and give much to those who were strong, and they would become great chiefs who ruled over their lands.

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: Man nì u nìn dámintì kunyabaantiiu poon ní báà ŋma nì u-damintiiku nì ku-taapu.
translated: We became friends among the villagers, and we were united with his friends and their relatives.

ref: The king sent for Joseph, who was quickly brought out of jail. He shaved, changed his clothes, and went to the king.
text: Niinee ní Faaroo tú bi yíiń Jooseefi. Ní bi nyántì-u kusarkadiin malaa, ní u kōol̀ ki kpántì u-wampeeŋkaati ní ki nín cá ubɔti nimbiin.
translated: Then Pharaoh had Joseph summoned. And they quickly brought him out of the dungeon, and he shaved and changed his clothes and then went in before Pharaoh.

ref: I saw seven fat, healthy cows come up out of the river, and they began feeding on the grass.
text: M yúee ní ki ká inasal iluli, i bí màlmal ki ŋān̄, ki nyánní dimɔɔlin ki dɔ́miń ki yú digbándi ki ŋmóò timool.
translated: I went and saw a big house. It was very beautiful, with many rooms, and built with strong walls and a good roof.

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Ní a yaa ká-iee, aa lì yii i náĺ tiba gba. I nín pée bí pu ŋgɔŋgɔmu ŋkpiimu puee, míǹ ní i gbíntìi bí. Ní m cúu fīntì.
translated: And they went home, they were preparing a big celebration for their mother. When they had finished all the preparations, then they called them. And I also arrived.

ref: Then seven other heads of grain came up. They were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Ní ndifiŋkpiimu mululi mun ugbuŋkoon fáá mu fáaḿee mun nyáń ki kútì idifin ipeekaayee pu.
translated: And strength, weakness, and greatness, everything, and smallness can cause difficulty.

ref: The seven skinny, ugly cows that came up later also stand for seven years, as do the seven bad heads of grain that were scorched by the desert wind. The dreams mean there will be seven years when there won't be enough grain.
text: Nnagɔŋgɔmu ŋkpiimu mululi mun páaǹní ipeekaayee boonee, taapu di sá abin aluli mun. Ní ndifiŋkpiimu mululi mun ugbuŋkoon fáá mu fáaḿee taapu sá ŋkùm abin aluli.
translated: The roots of the Nnagɔŋgɔmu tree are deep, reaching the depths of the earth, and so it is a strong tree. And if its roots are not deep, not reaching
